issue_title: Use DSC replicate number directly as seed for RNG
issue_body: In the current implementation, the default seed for RNG is `DSC_REPLICATE_ID + SCRIPT_ID` where `SCRIPT_ID` is the current `md5sum` hash of the script file, converted to long-int. But I'm a bit bothered by the fact that each time I change the script, the results change, because the seed changes ... I think we should just use `DSC_REPLICATE_ID` as the default. If users need fancier behavior they should just set their own seeds.

issue_title: Missing tests and their requirements
issue_body:
The following are SDK tests that are missing due to missing/infeasible requirements.

Myproxy activation. Requires a stable endpoint with a myproxy login for SDK Tester. (may be depreciated at some point anyways)

Login-flow. Requires automating a browser and SDK Tester's login credentials being made public.

Code exchange. Requires login-flow or a non expiring test code.

Live openid JWT decoding. Requires login-flow, a non expiring id_token, or an auth resource other than code exchange that responds with an an id_token.

Dependent token grants. Requires a resource server client and scopes to be set up.

Managed endpoint resources. Requires SDK Tester to have privileges on a managed endpoint, which would give public access to privileges on a managed endpoint.

issue_title: BaseChainDB no longer supports a header-only DB
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

`BaseChainDB._set_as_canonical_chain_head()` now expects the DB to contain transactions as well, and that breaks `LightChain` since it only syncs headers.

When there's a chain reorg, that method will try to [remove tx lookups from the DB](https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/master/evm/db/chain.py#L108-L110), but that crashes because we have no transactions in the DB

issue_title: No event logs are returned since 4.6.0
issue_body:
* Version: 4.6.0
* Python: 3.6.5
* OS: win


### What was wrong?

In version 4.6.0, filtering on contract events returns no logs when the contract address is set (using ganache-cli). It still fails after updating eth-abi to the alpha 2.0 version. Version 4.5.0 works.

The following code fails to return logs in 4.6.0:

```python
contract = web3.eth.contract(
    abi=abi,
    address=address
)
event_filter = contract.events.SomeEvent.createFilter(
    fromBlock=0,
    toBlock='latest'
)
event_filter.get_all_entries()
> []
```

The same code works fine in 4.5.0, returning an array of event dicts as expected.

Event logs are also returned in 4.6.0 when initializing the contract without an address:

```python
contract = web3.eth.contract(
    abi=abi
)
```

### How can it be fixed?

I have identified the issue is caused by the changes made in f1e8e915. Specifically these two lines:

https://github.com/ethereum/web3.py/commit/f1e8e91503153c9942db570ac88752fd63d0a278#diff-f1ec2154438a2971d779c561901b6591R27
https://github.com/ethereum/web3.py/commit/f1e8e91503153c9942db570ac88752fd63d0a278#diff-480e77d839a1bfb9e5557e00f9b9c7aeR16

Changing

https://github.com/ethereum/web3.py/blob/master/web3/utils/rpc_abi.py#L27

back to `'address': 'address'`, and 

https://github.com/ethereum/web3.py/blob/master/web3/middleware/normalize_request_parameters.py#L16

to `'address': apply_formatter_if(is_string, lambda x: x)})` solves the issue, though this is probably not the desired fix.

issue_title: Head-finding heuristic for label-equality sets needs some work
issue_body:
Issue #54 brought up an issue with linking quantifiers to the quantifiees in DMRs, but it revealed another problem: the head-finding heuristic doesn't work well with, e.g., coordinated modifiers (e.g. *The big and scary dog barks.*). The current heuristic is implemented as a sort (where secondary criteria are only employed for breaking ties) as follows:

1. prefer fewer outgoing args to other EPs in its label-equality set
2. prefer more incoming args from other EPs in the set
3. prefer quantifiers (e.g. *all* in *nearly all*)
4. finally sort by nodeid (proxy for surface order)

A useful criterion might be "prefer EPs closer to TOP", if TOP is defined. If TOP is *not* defined, then maybe we can consider the transitive /NEQ links (e.g. in the above example, "and" shares a label with "dog", and the args of "and" go to EPs whose args then go to "dog")

issue_title: Miniconda3 on Windows conda-forge update removes activate.bat
issue_body:
I installed Miniconda 3.6 on Windows 10 (64 bit) and right after ran the two commands

```
conda config --add channels conda-forge
conda update conda
```

This removed the `activate` and `deactivate` scripts from the `Miniconda3/Scripts` folder, such that the Anaconda Prompt was broken.

An interim solution was to make backups of the `activate` and `deactivate` scripts after the initial installation and copying them back into the `Scripts` folder after running updating to `conda-forge`.

But the question remains: Why do those scripts get lost?

issue_title: update conda.recipe for 4.6
Change in package specification solution
issue_body:
This issue relates to the version of `sphinx` being installed by `conda` when no specific version is required.

Running `conda create -n tmp python=3.3 numpy sphinx` suggests the following package specification solution:

```
The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    docutils:   0.12-py33_0  
    jinja2:     2.8-py33_0   
    markupsafe: 0.23-py33_0  
    numpy:      1.9.2-py33_0 
    openssl:    1.0.1k-1     
    pip:        7.1.2-py33_0 
    pygments:   2.0.2-py33_0 
    python:     3.3.5-4      
    readline:   6.2-2        
    setuptools: 18.1-py33_0  
    sphinx:     1.2.3-py33_0 
    sqlite:     3.8.4.1-1    
    system:     5.8-2        
    tk:         8.5.18-0     
    wheel:      0.24.0-py33_0
    xz:         5.0.5-0      
    zlib:       1.2.8-0
```

I would have expected to see `sphinx==1.3.1` installed since it is available in the repos. In fact going through one of my older Travis CI build logs reveals that this did work as expected (i.e. `sphinx==1.3.1` was installed) on Aug 21, 2015. That Travis CI build used `conda==3.16.0`. I have tried both `conda==3.17.0` and `conda==3.16.0`. Both versions now give the above suggested package specification.

Running `conda update -n tmp --all` afterwards does not help:

```

```

However, if I only ask for an update for `sphinx` (`conda update -n tmp sphinx`), I get the correct version:

issue_title: add timestamp metadata for tiebreaking conda-build 3 hashed packages
test and fix always_yes
Conda 4.2.x attempts to create lock files in readonly file-based channels and does not respect CONDA_ENVS_PATH
issue_body:
I am getting warnings and eventually an error after upgrading from 4.1.11 to 4.2.7 relating to lock files and packages attempting to write to shared read-only directories in our continuous build.

I modified `/shared/path/to/miniconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/lock.py` to find out where conda 4.2.x was putting lock files and found the following:

`WARNING conda.lock:touch(55): Failed to create lock, do not run conda in parallel processes [errno 13] /shared/path/to/vendor/conda/linux-64.pid24915.conda_lock`

`WARNING conda.lock:touch(55): Failed to create lock, do not run conda in parallel processes [errno 13] /shared/path/to/miniconda/pkgs/python-dateutil-2.4.2-py27_0.tar.bz2.pid24915.conda_lock`

`/shared/path/to` is read-only because it is used by multiple build-agents concurrently.

Additional I encountered the following error:

`CondaRuntimeError: Runtime error: RuntimeError: Runtime error: Could not open u'/shared/path/to/miniconda/pkgs/python-dateutil-2.4.2-py27_0.tar.bz2.part' for writing ([Errno 13] Permission denied: u'/shared/path/to/miniconda/pkgs/python-dateutil-2.4.2-py27_0.tar.bz2.part').`

In conda <= 4.1.11 we were able to set the `CONDA_ENVS_PATH` to have a shared installation of conda, but with separate package caches/lock files per build agent on our continuous build server, in order to avoid concurrent use of the package cache.

Two questions:
* How do we disable lock files being placed in read-only shared file-based channels.
* Does `CONDA_ENVS_PATH` no longer override the package cache/lock file directory from the root?

issue_title: Missing header for SAM output?
issue_body:
Not sure if this is intentional or not, but looks like when writing SAM (but not BAM) files the header is omitted. For instance, following the example from readthedocs: 

```
inbam = pysam.AlignmentFile("test/test_data/small.lmna.bam", "rb")
pairedreads = pysam.AlignmentFile("allpaired.bam", "wb", template=inbam)
for read in inbam.fetch():
     if read.is_paired:
             pairedreads.write(read)

pairedreads.close()
inbam.close()
```

...this works as expected and creates a correctly formatted BAM file.  However, replacing the second line with
```
pairedreads = pysam.AlignmentFile("allpaired.sam", "w", template=inbam)
```
results in a file with no header and just the reads. 

Seems a bit inconsistent, but perhaps there's a good reason for it?

issue_title: Segmentation fault after processing data in a different process
issue_body:
I have the following issue and do not know if it is my fault or an error in pysam:

I read the data from a samfile, store it in a buffer and then I hand over this buffer to a new Process of the multiprocessing library in python. Inside this new process I can access the data without any issue but if I return via a Queue a new buffer which contains elements of the old buffer if some requirements are fullfilled, I get a segmentation fault. 

I will show you a code example and I hope you can understand better what is going on.

```python

import pysam
import multiprocessing

def readData(pFileOneIterator, pFileTwoIterator, pNumberOfItemsPerBuffer):
   j = 0
   buffer_1 = []
   buffer_2 = []
   while j < pNumberOfItemsPerBuffer:
        j += 1
        try:
            mate1 = pFileOneIterator.next()
            mate2 = pFileTwoIterator.next()
        except StopIteration:
            break
        # if some_conditions on mate1 and mate2 fit
        buffer_1.append(mate1)
        buffer_2.append(mate2)
    return buffer_1, buffer_2

def process_data(pMateBuffer1, pMateBuffer2, pQueue):
    # process data
    i = 0
    buffer_out = []
    while i < len(pMateBuffer1):
        mate1 = pMateBuffer1[i]
        mate2 = pMateBuffer2[i]

        # if some conditions are fulfilled, add 
        buffer_out.append(mate1)
        buffer_out.append(mate2)
   # this access works
   print buffer_out[0].flag
   pQueue.put(buffer_out)
  
def main(args=None):
    # create alignment file
    str1 = pysam.Samfile('File1', 'rb')
    str2 = pysam.Samfile('File2', 'rb')

    buffer_1, buffer_2 = readData(str1, str2, 500000)
    queue = multiprocessing.Queue()
    p = multiprocessing.Process(target=process_data, kwargs=dict(
                    pMateBuffer1=buffer_1,
                    pMateBuffer2=buffer_2,
                    pQueue=queue
                    ))
    p.start()
    # let process compute and then get data back
    result = queue.get()
    # type can be seen:
    print type(result[0]) # prints <type 'pysam.libcalignedsegment.AlignedSegment'>
    # but this access raises a segementation fault
    print result[0].flag # Segmentation fault (core dumped)
    p.join()
```
Why can I copy the data from process A to B, but for some reasons it fails to copy all the data back from B to A? Am I doing something wrong if yes, what? For me it looks like that some pointers or references in the underlying data structure are deleted. 

Thanks a lot for your help!

issue_title: Add option to disable/lock file system tab by default
issue_body:
### Description

The file system tab works like a charm. But it slows down OCIDE if the files you open are on a (mapped) network share.

Once you've opened a file you can either close the File system tab (which would be restored when OCIDE is opened next time) or lock it. But you still have to do it each time you startup OCIDE.

Therefore I'd like to see the option to lock it by default (via preferences) and/or store an attribute for file system and navigation tab "open/closed" and only restore it on startup if it wasn't closed on last exit.

issue_title: [Enhancement] Update the binaries for Windows to GnuCOBOL 1.1
issue_body:
An "official" binary package with MinGW can be downloaded from http://sourceforge.net/projects/open-cobol/files/gnu-cobol/1.1/GnuCOBOL_1.1_MinGW_BDB_PDcurses_MPIR.7z

For a quite complete list of the differences between OC 1.1 and GC 1.1 see http://opencobol.add1tocobol.com/gnucobol/#what-are-the-differences-between-opencobol-1-1-and-gnucobol-1-1
There are some additional fixes, compare BUGS.txt in https://github.com/OpenCobolIDE/OpenCobol-Win32-MinGW/pull/1 to the new version included in the distribution.

This official MinGW package was build using the latests versions of prerequisites available:

    MinGW (minimal packages)
    GCC 4.8.1
    BDB 6.1.23
    PDcurses 3.4
    MPIR 2.7.0 (gmpcompat, with all processor optimization removed)

It doesn't contain "anything more" (not even the marvelous Programmer's Guide as it's not part of the GnuCOBOL project yet) and uses MPIR instead of GMP and is digitally signed (both the package and the GnuCOBOL parts).

If you need an unpacked version at git you're free to mirror it at OpenCobol-Win32-MinGW:master, too, but it seems a good idea to rename this project.

Please drop a note at https://sourceforge.net/p/open-cobol/discussion/contrib/thread/5d035f17/ when you have a release which includes this package, thank you.

BTW: What do you think about an installer that doesn't contain any GnuCOBOL binaries but adds an option to select a package for installation (MinGW, maybe MinGW64, Visual Studio 8/9/10/11/12/14 [either with both and 32/64 or with the option to choose] which would be downloaded from SourceForge [maybe as a fallback from gnu.org] and maybe all packages two times, one with BDB, one with VBISAM) - with a default to MinGW?

issue_title: ES S3 repo backup rotation
issue_body:
Hi

I'm using ES 2.1.1 and curator 3.5.
In addition, im using curator as my auto increment cluster backup (cron job which running every hour)
I read today that ES doesn't reccomand using same repo for long time since the recovery will take longer than without snapshots.

The idea is to create weekly \ monthly repository and make snapshots until the next week \ month come and then rotate the S3 repository and create new (full) backup.

I see that for managing ES backup repos there is this tool: `es_repo_mgr` which will be added also to cron job.

My question is what is your recommendation for repo rotate and how can I set dynamic repo in the cron script?

Thanks
D.

issue_title: ChoicesEnum with integers not comparable with itself or int
issue_body:
* Choices Enum version: 0.1.7
* Python version: 3.6.2
* Operating System: Linux

## Description

I'm porting existing Django code to use choiceenum. In this code, there is two use cases that causes comparision of the `choicesenum.django.fields.EnumIntegerField` values:

- Sorting in Python instead of the DB (comparing `EnumIntegerField` to `EnumIntegerField`)
- Running `.full_clean()` on the model, causing comparision between the `ChoicesEnum` type and `-2**31`, which is probably the lowest value that can be stored in the database field used by `EnumIntegerField` when running on PostgreSQL.

## What I Did

### Data model

``` python
from choicesenum import ChoicesEnum
from choicesenum.django.fields import EnumIntegerField

from django.db import models


class ProductNumber(ChoicesEnum):
    UNDEFINED = None
    COKE = 10
    HOT_DOG = 20

class Product(models.Model):
    product_number = EnumIntegerField(
        enum=ProductNumber,
        default=ProductNumber.UNDEFINED,
        unique=True,
        null=True)
   # ... other irrelevant fields ...
```

### Example: Sorting in Python

``` python
items = sorted(items, key=lambda item: (item.start_date, item.product.product_number)
```

This would cause an error like this:

```
TypeError: '<' not supported between instances of 'ProductNumber' and 'ProductNumber'
```

### Example: Model validation through `full_clean()`

```
product.full_clean()
```

This would cause an error like this raised from `django/core/validators.py:325`:

```
TypeError: '<' not supported between instances of 'ProductNumber' and 'int'
```

### Possible solution: subclass int

My first attempt at resolving this was to subclass int, like `IntEnum`:

``` python
class ProductNumber(int, ChoicesEnum):
    UNDEFINED = 0
    COKE = 10
    HOT_DOG = 20
```

Though, this immediately crashed with:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File ".../lib/python3.6/enum.py", line 199, in __new__
    enum_member = __new__(enum_class, *args)
  File ".../lib/python3.6/site-packages/choicesenum/enums.py", line 10, in__new__
    obj = object.__new__(cls)
TypeError: object.__new__(ProductNumber) is not safe, use ProductNumber.__new__()
```

issue_title: [service] Non-provisioned VM blocks Image deletion; Image doesn't take a lease on the backing blob
issue_body:
I found what look like at least two different service issues today.

Basically, I've got an Image that I can't delete, and whose backing blob no longer exists. Neither of these seem like situations I should be able to get into.

Note: I think some of this is specifically because the VM I booted didn't report successful provisioning to the platform, so Azure thinks the VM is still booting. It's unclear though, why this would block the Image deletion. Presumably at this time, the Image was copied to a new blob for the OS Disk... but maybe there are backend COW optimizations...?

## Repro

1. Create an RG "aznixos"
2. Create a Storage account and container in that RG
3. Upload a VHD to the storage container
4. Make an "Image" from the VHD Blob.
5. Create a new RG "colemick-nixos-20180423"
6. Create a VM using the Image from the other resource group
7. Later, decide you don't want that image anymore.
8. Delete the `aznixos` resource group
---

**Expected**: The resource group is deleted.

**Alternative Expectation**: The resource group is NOT deleted, because the Image took a lease on the backing blob, which would then prevent the 

**Actual**: The RG fails to delete after sometime (an hour?). Manually attempting to delete the Image results in this error: (which is amusing, at this point in time, the backing blob for the Image doesn't exist, so the Image is probably useless anyway)

```
Operation Not Allowed
Operation 'Image Delete' is not allowed since the Virtual Machines 'colemick-nixosdev' are being provisioned using the Image 'nixos-image-16.09.1694.019dcc3-x86_64-linux-vhd'.
```

### Issues

From my deduction, it seems like there might be three issues:

1. Why didn't the Image take a lease out on the blob?
2. Why does the VM block image deletion? It appears that a user can block Image deletion by booting a VM that doesn't complete provisionig.
3. Why does ARM just keep retrying this delete?

While looking into this, I've noticed other oddities:

1. I'd noticed this literally all the time when using Azure, but hte Activity Log is completely unreliable. There was no evidence of my attempt to delete the `aznixos` group in the Activity Log, despite having done in multiple times. I would've assumed the Activity Log accuracy would be important, but it just doesn't seem that way.

2. The resource group just reverted back to "Success!" after the deletion failed. That just doesn't seem quite right.

cc: @yugangw-msft

issue_title: Network Watcher - Topology - error: __init__() takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**

Reporting with permission from user

error: __init__() takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given

I tried it without the –subnet option and got the same error. As you can see, the portal renders the topology using the same resource group and VNet names. Tried it this morning ONLY using –resource-group, and it worked!  It should work with the other options too though, or they should be removed. I put two VNets in the resource group and the command returned the topology for both (as expected), but if I wanted it for only one VNet, it would be nice to be able to specify.

![cli_bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20936882/39879962-273aa976-5431-11e8-8cb7-a65b0836f884.jpg)

**To Reproduce**
See above.

**Expected behavior**
If this is a client error, the client should report back to the user with proper guidance on why the request failed.

**Environment summary**
CloudShell - unknown version

**Additional context**
@jimdial reported the issue.

issue_title: [KeyVault] There is no way to list pending certificates
issue_body:
Certificates that are in the process of being created by taking a pending CSR to a CA cannot be discovered.

The `az keyvault certificate list` command only returns certificates which have been fully created.
There is no API call to list certificate operations.

As a result, there is no `az keyvault certificate pending list` command (but there should be).

The problem is that if you are in the process of getting a certificate from a CA, when you get the certificate and try to use `az keyvault certificate merge`, if you don't remember you used, you will not be able to complete the process. Or, if you decide NOT to get the certificate from the CA and abort the process, you can delete the operation _only if_ you remember the name (you ALSO have to delete the certificate object, because the two are independent objects, but you can't list either of them).

This issue is also applicable to Xplat and Powershell.

cc/ @amarzavery

issue_title: Return Value of Calculate Inverse Kinematics
issue_body:
The _calculateInverseKinematics_ function does not return the correct joint configuration to the corresponding point in task space. _calculateInverseKinematics_ only returns a **joint configuration that is closer to specified point in task space than the current joint configuration**. Therefore, the return values of the inverse kinematics are dependent on the current joint configuration. This configuration dependency was already mentioned in issue #1249. Only if the current joint configuration is close to the IK-solution, the correct joint configuration is returned.

**Is the behavior desired or not?** 
If this behavior is desired, could you please specify the behavior in the pybullet quick start guide, as this is not the behavior I expected. 

This state dependency can be observed in the [inverse kinematic example](https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3/blob/master/examples/pybullet/examples/inverse_kinematics.py). As shown below the Task-space controller can follow the cirlce in task-space. However, n-iterations, which is equivalent to calling the _calculateInverseKinematics_ function n-times, are required to compute the correct desired joint configuration. 

![inversekinematics_iiwa_circle](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10160295/31551717-b21b9dde-b035-11e7-9e45-7e3e43fb3bbb.png)

More closely, the IK computation needs around 5 iterations to converge to the correct joint configuration. As this example does not simulate the joints but sets the joints (via _resetJointState_) to the IK solution in every iteration, I would expect the convergence to the correct configuration from the first time-step.   

![inversekinematics_iiwa_circle_1s](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10160295/31551723-b5176590-b035-11e7-8177-1981a7941fe2.png)


The plots were generated using the [inverse kinematic example](https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3/blob/master/examples/pybullet/examples/inverse_kinematics.py) with the following parameters:

rp = [0,0,0,0,0, 0,0]
useOrientation = 0
useSimulation = 0
useRealTimeSimulation = 0

issue_title: Remove reader parameter from request.multipart()
issue_body:
The parameter exists from the very beginning, it is declared as:
```
    async def multipart(self, *, reader=multipart.MultipartReader):
```
I found that we can drop it: `reader` is always `multipart.MultipartReader`, aiohttp doesn't provide an alternative multipart reader implementation.

I have a problem with adding type hints to the method. Now it can return anything which is returned by `reader(headers, content)` call.  Dropping the parameter allows specifying the method return type without any functionality lose.

@kxepal maybe you have a comment?

issue_title: Unexplained CancelledErrors Over Extended Period
issue_body:
## Long story short

I am experiencing intermittent but frequent unexplained CancelledErrors under load in AIOHTTP2. I'm not sure if I am doing something wrong or their is an issue. Looking at server logs and metrics, I'm not seeing any evidence this is a downstream hangup on aiohttp. Within the aiohttp code, I catch the CancelledError and timed the aiohttp requests, and the cancellation is always immediate such as 0.001 seconds  (timex).

issue_title: Document exceptions
issue_body:
## Long story short

Now it's non-obvious what each exception means. E.g. I get `ClientResponseError`, looking at the code it seems that it means code >= 400, but it can also be caused by `HttpProcessingError`. Also, all the fields should be documented otherwise people will look into code and use everything they find, making all that everything a subject to API stability.

Another thing I would love to have is a way to easily separate permanent from temporary (retriable) errors.  

## Expected behaviour

A section in the docs with list, meanings/causes and public attributes.

## Actual behaviour

Nothing in the docs on exceptions.

issue_title: Forbid usage of qweb.add_template(), use ajax.loadXML()
issue_body:
Hi there linter pros! This issue and specifically this comment is what I'm referring to: https://github.com/odoo/odoo/issues/20821#issuecomment-358577401

Summarizing, there's the common pattern (also used by Odoo itself) of synchronously loading client-side xml qweb templates by using `qweb.add_template('/addon/static/xml/sometemplate.xml')`, but the correct way of doing this would be by using `ajax.loadXML('addon/static/xml/sometemplate.xml', core.qweb)`.

The difference is that it is being loaded synchronously with the 1st method, and asynchronously with the 2nd. So, the 2nd makes the whole website more responsive, and returns a `$.Deferred()` that can be chained to avoid race conditions.

Having a linter that forbids using that method would be just freaking awesome!

issue_title: uatest results in 400 error POST to on /uploads/.../complete
issue_body:
## Looks like there has been a change in the DukeDS API.
### Release DukeDS server: 
When you POST to create an __upload__ it has optional hash and algorithm parameters. 
ddsclient has always filled these in.

### Uatest DukeDS server: 
When you POST to __upload__  no longer has optional hash and algorithm parameters. 
These are now required parameters to the PUT to complete the __upload__.

When you attempt to upload to uatest it results in this error:

```
Error 400 on /uploads.../complete Reason:hash is missing, hash[value] is missing, hash[algorithm] is missing 
```
### Fix
I'm going to try passing the hash and algorithm to both endpoints so we can be functional for both uatest and dev.

Use the @bgruening method to [build a list of dependencies](http://nbviewer.ipython.org/github/bgruening/notebooks/blob/master/R/extract_all_dependencies_from_an_r_package.ipynb), download the dependencies (planemo has code to do this), and upload them to Github (planemo has code for publishing Gists and handling github credentials in `~/.planemo.yml`).

See also: https://wiki.galaxyproject.org/SetUpREnvironment

issue_title: Decide APIv1 endpoints
issue_body:
My understanding is that APIv1 endpoints are pretty much decided (in offline discussions). I'm documenting the current state here.

@jcnelson said on Slack 

```
endpoints are:
* registering a name: `POST /api/v1/names` with JSON payload `{'name': '...'}`
* transferring a name to an address:  `PUT /api/v1/names/:name/owner` with JSON payload `{'owner': '...'}`
* getting the state of all pending transactions:  `GET /api/v1/blockchain/bitcoin/pending`
* getting all names owned by an address: `GET /api/v1//addresses/:address/names`
* getting the address of a name, as well as its zonefile: `GET /api/v1/names/:name`
* getting the address of the owner and wallet of the blockstack node: `GET /api/v1/node/wallet/public
```

I'd also like to identify the **must-haves** from **nice-to-have**, so we can focus on the import endpoints first.

issue_title: Threading blockstackd
issue_body: As of now, `blockstackd` handles requests single-threaded -- It may be possible to significantly improve the responsiveness of `blockstackd` through adding threading into the SimpleXMLRPCServer. However, responsiveness during the handling of database _updates_ will still be low -- as read operations in sqlite are serialized when a write lock is held -- we could enable WAL mode, which would allow reads to happen concurrently with writes, however, that may not be necessary.

issue_title: Refactor out common response handling code
issue_body:
The following hunk of code shows up in many places throughout NailGun, especially `nailgun.entity_mixins` and `nailgun.entities`:

```python
response.raise_for_status()
if (synchronous is True and
        response.status_code == ACCEPTED):
    return _poll_task(response.json()['id'], self._server_config)
if response.status_code == NO_CONTENT:
    return
return response.json()
```

It would be nice if this idiom could be refactored out into a helper function.

issue_title: Xref should generate mappings for check-cast opcode
issue_body:
This can be treated like a class call.

For example, loading a webview:
```
        5  (00000016) invoke-virtual      v2, v0, Lcom/example/test/MainActivity;->findViewById(I)Landroid/view/View;
        6  (0000001c) move-result-object  v0
        7  (0000001e) check-cast          v0, Landroid/webkit/WebView;
```

In this case there is no knowledge what has been loaded, because we currently do not resolve the IDs into the R class. But we can see the check-cast with the type WebView, therefore we know that this class is used.

we should add that to the _create_xref method.

issue_title: Allow for custom hook callers?
issue_body:
In #133 there was a request to be able to provide a custom `_HookCaller` (in this case in order to be able to warn on deprecated hook calls - since addressed via #138). 

This feature may be quite useful in the future not only in its ability to allow users to customize the `pluggy` runtime but also if we want to eventually support async / coroutine based plugins (this can be done loop agnostic these days with something like [`multio`](https://github.com/theelous3/multio) and/or [`trio-asyncio`](https://github.com/python-trio/trio-asyncio)).

I'd like to get feedback on this.

To start it would mean that we would make a simple change where `PluginManager` can take a `hook_caller_type` as input during instantiation.

issue_title: Does pytest-cov co-works with pytest if a test is marked with @pytest.mark.skipif?
issue_body:
If a test is disabled due to skipif mark provided by pytest, I think it shouldn't check the coverage as well.
But I can't find any option in pytest-cov that makes it available.
Only options provided is adding "exclude list" into .coveragerc or "# pragma: no cover"  for code in each file.
```python
@pytest.mark.skipif('moduleA' not in sys.modules,
                    reason="requires moduleA library")
def test_A(self):
        # Some test code with assert
```
If it doesn't co-work with pytest in such case, does pytest-cov support an conditional api for excluding  test  as "pytest.mark.skipif" does?
Thanks.

issue_title: feature request - open html log with closed details
issue_body:
Hi there,

issue_title: LTM policies cannot be drafted after they have been published
issue_body:
This kinda throws a wrench into the ability to modify them.

When trying to create drafts, from an already published policy, the following error raises

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "ok.py", line 25, in <module>
    ok = mgmt.tm.ltm.policys.policy.create(**params)
  File "/Users/trupp/src/trupp/f5-common-python/f5/bigip/resource.py", line 974, in create
    return self._create(**kwargs)
  File "/Users/trupp/src/trupp/f5-common-python/f5/bigip/tm/ltm/policy.py", line 83, in _create
    self = super(Policy, self)._create(**kwargs)
  File "/Users/trupp/src/trupp/f5-common-python/f5/bigip/resource.py", line 941, in _create
    response = session.post(_create_uri, json=kwargs, **requests_params)
  File "/Users/trupp/src/envs/ansible-2.0/lib/python2.7/site-packages/icontrol/session.py", line 272, in wrapper
    raise iControlUnexpectedHTTPError(error_message, response=response)
icontrol.exceptions.iControlUnexpectedHTTPError: 400 Unexpected Error: Bad Request for uri: https://localhost:10443/mgmt/tm/ltm/policy/
Text: u'{"code":400,"message":"01070734:3: Configuration error: Published policy \\"/Common/published-policy16\\" exists, use create-draft to create a draft copy .","errorStack":[],"apiError":3}'
```

_modify and _update do not work if the policy isnt a draft, and if you load a draft and then call modify and publish.

For example,

```
params = dict(
    name='published-policy18',
    partition='Common',
    subPath='Drafts',
    strategy='first-match'
)
mgmt = BigIpRoot('localhost', 'admin', 'admin', port=10443)
ok = mgmt.tm.ltm.policys.policy.create(**params)

ok.publish()
```

The above creates a draft and publishes it. If however, you want to modify this, you need to draft that policy. The only want to draft a policy, is with `create` because `modify` and `update` prevent any editing.

https://github.com/F5Networks/f5-common-python/blob/development/f5/bigip/tm/ltm/policy.py#L97
https://github.com/F5Networks/f5-common-python/blob/development/f5/bigip/tm/ltm/policy.py#L113

If, however you create the draft of that same policy name and then try to publish it,

```
params = dict(
    name='published-policy18',
    partition='Common',
    subPath='Drafts',
    strategy='first-match'
)
ok = mgmt.tm.ltm.policys.policy.create(**params)

ok.publish()
```

You get the above mentioned error

```
Text: u'{"code":400,"message":"01070734:3: Configuration error: Published policy \\"/Common/published-policy18\\" exists, use create-draft to create a draft copy .","errorStack":[],"apiError":3}'
```

issue_title: Magic for functions
issue_body:
One of the limitations of MATLAB is that you can't define functions in the REPL, they must be in a separate file.  This seems to carry over to this kernel:

    function a=test()
       disp('test')
    end

Results in:

    Error: Function definitions are not permitted in this context.

It would be nice if there was a magic for functions that would automatically save the function with the same name defined by the function and put the directory the file is in the path.

I know this can be done manually using %%file magic and then manually adding the current directory (or whatever directory you use) to the session's path, but this seems to be such a basic feature I think it warrants a dedicated macro (I know the lack of the feature is primarily MATLAB's fault).

A possible call signature would be as follows:

    %%function [-t|--directory==DIRECTORY|--parent==PARENT]

`-t` would save the file to a temporary directory (defined by pythons' `tempfile` module), `--directory` would save the file to `DIRECTORY`, and `--parent` would store the file as a subfunction of parent function `PARENT`.  These options are mutually exclusive.  I think the default directory would be a subdirectory of the current directory, perhaps `functions`.

issue_title: Support field names that aren't valid Python identifiers
issue_body:
I want to support a field called "from", but I can't do this in marshmallow because it would cause a syntax error:

```python
class MySchema(Schema):
	from = fields.Nested(OtherSchema, attribute="my_attribute")
```

This got me thinking -- marshmallow is kind of backwards, isn't it?  The field that comes in from the outside world is the one that has to be specified as a valid Python identifier, and the field that you're actually going to work with in your code is specified as a string.  Shouldn't they be swapped?  I'm sure other folks are going to want to parse in or write out a value that isn't a valid Python identifier sometimes.

```python
class MySchema(Schema):
	my_attribute = fields.Nested(OtherSchema, field="from")
```

This could even be made backward compatible with existing Marshmallow stuff, like so:

- If neither "field" nor "attribute" are specified, the field and attribute values are both the same as the lvalue, so there's no issue.
- If "attribute" is specified, the lvalue is used for "field"
- if "field" is specified, the lvalue is used for "attribute"

Probably harder for you to actually implement this sorta thing though, but it's a thought.

However, it doesn't appear to do any validation:

```

```

Is this expected behavior?  Are there some types of errors that it will catch?

issue_title: [Question] Many-to-many relationships
issue_body:
Is there a standard way to handle many-to-many Python object relationships with this library? Nested fields obviously won't work because they would create duplicate objects where you want just one object that is referred to by several others.

It is possible to handle this manually, for example by using some sort of unique `key` (or `name` or `id`) field and translating lists of objects into lists of these keys, but this is kind of awkward.

I'm thinking of something like a `Linked` field type that works like the `Nested` one, except it doesn't lead to the construction of anything and only validates that the linked object exists - sorry, I don't know enough about the library to assess whether this is possible at all. In the output dictionary, the link could be represented by the method I mentioned above.

Thank you in advance for your input.

issue_title: Expectation/Request: "default" implies "required"
issue_body:
I've seen the previous request to make `required=True` by default, and I don't disagree. Slightly more surprising, though, was that I can specify a `default` value but not have that value filled in if the field is not also `required`. I'd suggest that `default=<not 'missing'>` should imply `required=True`.

Note: the context wherein I noticed the default value wasn't getting filled in was in a validator. It's possible the behavior is localized to this context, and that the default value is set in e.g. the decorated function. I haven't tested that, though, because in my current usage the validation fails (missing default value) if I don't set `default=<value>, required=True`.

issue_title: Duplication issue with query using filter on M2M relation in Meta class for Resource.
issue_body:
Hi all, I've encountered an odd issue that results in the output from a GET call containing duplicate objects due to the manner in which the Django query is constructed and yields rows.

For off, I'm using Django 1.7.9 with Tastypie 0.12.1. Database is Posgresql 9.4

The setup required to duplicate this issue is a little involved.

First off, the models:
```
class A(models.Model):
    pass

class B(models.Model):
    instances_of_a = models.ManyToManyField(A, related_name='instances_of_b', null=True, blank=True)
```

now for the problematic Resource definition:
```
class AResource(ModelResource):
    class Meta:
        queryset = A.objects.all()

class BResource(ModelResource):
    instances_of_a = fields.ManyToManyField(AResource, 'instances_of_a', full=True, null=True)

    class Meta:
        queryset = B.objects.filter(instances_of_a__isnull=False)
```

When a GET is performed against BResource, any instances of B that are associated with more than one A object via the instances_of_a M2M relation will be returned multiple times in the response from BResource. The number of times an instance of B will be returned is equal to the number of A objects associated with it by the instances_of_a M2M relation.

The reason for this is due to the SQL Django generates to produce the Queryset. The SQL in question looks like this
```
SELECT
"database_schema_name"."id"
FROM "B"
 INNER JOIN "A"
   ON ("B"."id" =
       "B_A_Assn"."B_id")
WHERE ("B_A_Assn"."A_id" IS NOT NULL);
```

The result of this SQL is the issue describe above wherein there will be duplicate items in the queryset.

Probably the simplest solution to this is to not use a related filter in the fashion described but I would image it would be possible to detect for this kind of condition and handle it?

issue_title: Overflow warning from pvlib.clearsky.haurwitz
issue_body:
The Haurwitz model takes apparent_zenith as its argument.  pvlib ephemeris calculations can return zenith>90 for times after sunset. The Haurwitz model includes this term: 
`np.exp(-0.059/cos_zenith)`
which causes a numerical overflow when cos_zenith is small and negative, i.e., apparent_zenith is greater than but close to 90.
The current Haurwitz model code obscures the warning by 
` clearsky_ghi[clearsky_ghi < 0] = 0`
`clearsky_ghi < 0` occurs whenever `cos_zenith<0`
I propose that we apply the Haurwitz formula only for `cos_zenith>0` and set `clearsky_ghi=0` otherwise.
The function output would remain unchanged but the overflow condition would be handled.

issue_title: `write_only` field included in response
issue_body: Declaring a `write_only` on a serializer should not change the response. Currently, `write_only` fields are included in the response with a `null` value.

issue_title: Document include options for Compound Documents
issue_body:
With #250 pulled in we now have options to include relations to form compound documents:

1. Include resources related to the primary data by **default**.
2. Include resources related to the primary data through use of a request **parameter** which customizes which related resources should be returned

### Example Current Implementation
1. **default** include
```python
class UserSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
    name = serializers.CharField(max_length=200)
    # PostSerializer included directly on the serializer
    posts = PostSerializer(many=True, read_only=True)
 
    class JSONAPIMeta:
        # included_resources specifys resources to include by default
        included_resources = ['posts']
```
2) **param** include
```python
class UserSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
    name = serializers.CharField(max_length=200)
    # PostSerializer NOT included directly on the serializer, ResourceRelatedField is 
    # specified as default
    posts = relations.ResourceRelatedField(
            source='post_set', many=True, read_only=True) 

    # specifies serializer to use when passing in the `include` param
    included_serializers = {
        'posts': PostSerializer,
    }
```

We should probably document this. We can even copy the examples above with a bit of explanation.

issue_title: The get_resource_type_from_serializer bug
issue_body:
django-rest-framework-json-api v2.0.0-beta.2
I have defined `resource_name` attribut into Meta class of serializer:
```python 
class MySerializer(serializers.Serializer):
    email = serializers.EmailField(required=True)
    class Meta:
        resource_name = 'user'
```
In this case the `get_resource_type_from_serializer` will always raise AttributeError, because there isn't defined the `model` attribute.
Code of `get_resource_type_from_serializer`:
```python
def get_resource_type_from_serializer(serializer):
    return getattr(
        serializer.Meta,
        'resource_name',
        get_resource_type_from_model(serializer.Meta.model))
```

issue_title: How to inject something on a function argument?
issue_body:
First of all, great work!

I'm coming from the PHP world where I'm used to having method level injections so if something only used in 1-2 methods then it doesn't necessarily need to get injected on the class level. I trying to figure out how could I do this but I'm getting lost in the documentation.

I wish to do something like this:
```python
from dependency_injector.containers import DeclarativeContainer
from dependency_injector.providers import Factory
from django.http import HttpRequest, HttpResponse
from django.views.generic import View


class MyContainer(DeclarativeContainer):
    response = Factory(HttpResponse)


class MyView(MyContainer, View):
    
    def get(self, request: HttpRequest, response: HttpResponse)
        return response.write('Hello, World!')
```

So something like upon calling a method the unprovided arguments get instances from the factories based on their name.

Does something similar exists in this framework?

issue_title: Update the save queue functionality
issue_body:
The queue saving functionality is broken as it tries to create playlists using the 'local' scheme rather than 'm3u' (or 'm3u8'). 
* Tries to create the playlist using the 'local' scheme, which fails.
* Instead then creates the playlist using the next available playlist provider, on my system this happens to be Spotify

Next time, it checks for a 'local' or 'm3u' playlist matching the name and doesn't find one. So creates another unique playlist with the same name (which Spotify has no problem with).

It also uses the deprecated `PlaylistsController.filter` function.

issue_title: Problem with parse_smtlib2_string
Regexp equality with two literals gives invalid model
issue_body:
I'm aware that regexp support is in really, really early stages and only intended to be used for str.in.re queries right now, so this report is mostly informative.

Right now if I try regexp equality between two regexp literals, I can get invalid models. For example:

```
(set-option :model-validate true)

issue_title: Relocation using local symbol
issue_body:
in relocation.py `relocate()` starts with a symbol resolution leading to `resolve_symbol()`

if the symbol has `is_static` set, the function stops, if not it tries to resolve the symbol using its name.

Problems then come when there are multiple symbols with the same name which is allowed for local symbols (and used at least in linux kernel modules).

> [STB_LOCAL](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E19683-01/816-1386/chapter6-79797/index.html)
Local symbol. These symbols are not visible outside the object file containing their definition. Local symbols of the same name can exist in multiple files without interfering with each other.

This leads to Relocation having a wrong `resolvedby` attribute set in `resolve()`. 

As we don't see how STB_LOCAL symbols could be resolved by another symbol, we use this quick & dirty patch:
``` diff
@@ -33,7 +33,7 @@ class Relocation(object):
         if self.resolved:
             return True
 
-        if self.symbol.is_static:
+        if self.symbol.is_static or self.symbol.binding == 'STB_LOCAL':
             # A static symbol should only be resolved by itself.
             self.resolve(self.symbol)
             return True
```
Works for us but maybe we are breaking other use cases. 
Not sure exactly how `is_static` is defined but maybe a STB_LOCAL symbol qualifies.

issue_title: Question: Caller-Simprocedure
issue_body:
Hello, not a bug this time around - just a short question.

I want to skip indirect-calls in ARM32, e.g. `BLX R3` with R3 being an address only known at runtime. 

A working solution is 

```python
project.hook(addr=0x400e5b, hook=indirect_blx_hook, length=2)

def indirect_blx_hook(state):
    if state.addr % 2 == 0:
        state.regs.r14 = state.regs.pc + 4 # set LR
    else:
        state.regs.r14 = state.regs.pc + 2 # set LR
```

This method requires an additional length parameter in `hook` which, ofc has to be `thumb/arm` sensitive.

My question is, if it is possible to address this more generically with the Stubs proecudure 'Caller' which gets the return unconstrained hook as target. Or any other more appropriate solution. Couldn't get it to work

Greetings,
Andy

issue_title: Constrained bytes in files vs. bytes in symbolic variables difference
issue_body:
When a byte in a file is constrained the bit index in the constraint starts at 0, e.g. file[7:0] for the first byte of the file. When a byte in a variable is constrained the bit index starts at VARIABLE_LENGTH-1, e.g. arg[39:32] for a variable that is 40 bits long. This appears to be a recent change. 

I have code that attempts to answer the question is byte X in file/arg constrained. It would help if there was some consistency in how bytes are represented as constraints.

issue_title: Feature Request: enhance output for external trackers
issue_body:
Today, if you query for information (from the cli) and use 'external_bug' the data you get back is in the BZ's [array of 1](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/docs/en/html/api/Bugzilla/WebService/Bug.html) format, which gets translated, to a dictionary of sub-elements. 

Because of this, the --outputformats inability to parse/define what (subfields) you see from this, the output is not very usable, or parsable!

Is there a way, [like what we do with CVE/comments](https://github.com/python-bugzilla/python-bugzilla/blob/master/bin/bugzilla#L655-L690) to improve this?

Mainly the (fields): 
- [ ] ext_bz_bug_id
- [ ] ext_status
- [ ] ext_description

issue_title: should we introduce a ProxyError
issue_body:
Would people be objecting to introducing a ProxyError exception or even one called UserError?

This would allow us to raise exceptions which are defined as being due to a typo or mistake or bad code entered at the IPython prompt, rather than a bug in Ganga.

I'd like to make the prompt a little more friendly as some people associate the trace with something going horribly wrong rather than the trace being there to help them.
(I know a lot of people simply didn't care for the old logging information for instance)

Or, even should we disable the trace from a normal production release?

issue_title: unicode bug in DAVIDenrich
issue_body:
From `AGEpy/AGEpy.py` in `DAVIDenrich` at line [427](https://github.com/mpg-age-bioinformatics/AGEpy/blob/master/AGEpy/AGEpy.py#L427)
```
for f in david_fields:
    line.append(str(d[f]))
```

the `str` call fails:

```
UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode character u'\ufffd' in position 19: ordinal not in range(128)
```

One solution is to convert to unicode and envode as ascii, as in:

```
for f in david_fields:
    line.append(unicode(d[f]).encode('ascii','ignore'))
```

issue_title: Excluding fields using a ResourceAdapter with the exclude attribute doesn't work
issue_body:
I have a resource adapter that excludes a field just like the documentation suggested:
```python
import odin
from odin.codecs import json_codec

class MyResource(odin.Resource):
  foo = odin.StringField()
  bar = odin.StringField()

class MyResourceAdapter(odin.ResourceAdapter):
  exclude = ['foo']

print(json_codec.dumps(MyResourceAdapter(MyResource(foo='1', bar='1'))))
```
prints the following JSON:
```json
{
  "foo": "1",
  "bar": "1",
  "$": "__main__.MyResource"
}
```

Passing exclude as a constructor argument works as expected as proven by the following code snippet:
```python
print(json_codec.dumps(MyResourceAdapter(MyResource(foo='1', bar='1'), exclude=['foo'])))
```

prints the following JSON:
```json
{
  "bar": "1",
  "$": "__main__.MyResource"
}
```

This could be a documentation issue but I'm not sure what is the right usage of the API to fix that.

issue_title: API should allow multiple requests to be stubbed at the same time
issue_body:
Multiple interactions should be allowed to be set up at the same time. The /delete call should be called before the test, not before each interaction is set up. 

See this example using bash here: https://gist.github.com/bethesque/9d81f21d6f77650811f4

https://github.com/pact-foundation/pact-python/blob/master/pact/pact.py#L119

```python
def setup(self):
        """Configure the Mock Service to ready it for a test."""
        try:
            payload = {
                'description': self._description,
                'provider_state': self._provider_state,
                'request': self._request,
                'response': self._response
            }

            resp = requests.delete(
                self.uri + '/interactions', headers=self.HEADERS)

            assert resp.status_code == 200, resp.content
            resp = requests.post(
                self.uri + '/interactions',
                headers=self.HEADERS, json=payload)

issue_title: Testing of `console` logging option
issue_body:
This is a test of the console logging option added to server.conf in [Story 2548](https://pulp.plan.io/issues/2548). These are not the best tests, but to really benefit from the value of this story you have to run the processes in a docker container.

Each test changes the config value `log_type` in the `[server]` section of `/etc/pulp/server.conf`.


### Test the default is `syslog`:

1. Have `log_type` commented so that the default is used.
2. Start Pulp normally and observe logs being sent to syslogd.


### Test the `console` option

This test could be better.

1. Set `log_type` to `console`
2. Start Pulp normally and observe logs going to syslog. This happens also when console is used because systemd does that when it starts processes that don't send logs to somewhere other than systemd.

Note: When I tested this in my dev environment, I added a statement that assured me the new `console` codepath was taken.


### Test an invalid value

1. Set `log_type` to `asdf`
2. Start Pulp normally and observe the `log_type not properly set. Defaulting to syslog.` in the logs and syslog is being used.

issue_title: Choice of value loss function in policy gradients
issue_body:
Hi,

first of all the obvious. Thank you so much for releasing this code :)

I'm reading the source for various policy gradient methods. The focus seems to be very much on making the policy updates smart: Tradeoffs in variance / bias for advantage estimators and good ways to control the policy gradients.

But what about the value function. That seems to be always mse based on some n-step TD-lambda estimation of the value. Which should be very high variance. And then mse can blow up.

Is this a problem ?
Have you tried something like huber loss, to make the value gradients more manageable ?

issue_title: Rest Framework SerializerMutation Error - NoneType is not callable
Avoid calling count unless it is required
issue_body:
It is checking the count, but isn't using it for anything AFAICT except `hasNextPage`

```
[4.490] SELECT COUNT(*) AS "__count"
FROM "nsproperties_apt"

[0.159] SELECT "nsproperties_apt"."id",
```

This is a massive performance hit on postgresql.  If I am selecting only 10 items and I don't ask for the count then it should not call count. Even if the number of records is < 10 it still calls count.

For hasNextPage you can just iterate one past.

Related to #162 - it would be nice to get totalCount when we need it, but only pay the performance price then.

issue_title: Add a way to serve custom properties on model classes
issue_body:
In SQLAlchemy, you often want to provide additional custom properties on the model classes, like this:
```python
class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)

    @property
    def full_name(self):
        return self.first_name + ' ' + self.last_name
```
Unfortunately, such properties are currently not made accessible in GraphQL by graphene_sqlalchemy. While there is a way to exclude existing column properties using the Meta attributes `exclude_fields` and `only_fields`, there is no way to include custom properties such as `full_name` from the above model.

One possible solution is to use [hybrid properties](http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/orm/extensions/hybrid.html) which are now supported by graphene_sqlalchemy since 2.0. However, "ordinary" properties should also be supported. Often you dont really want or need hybrid properties - it is not always necessary or feasible to provide the property on the class level. Also, the conversion of hybrid properties currently has the limitation that it always generates `String` fields.

So I believe we need some mechanism for proxying arbitrary properties from SQLAlchemy to Graphene objects. I'm not yet sure whether all custom public properties (i.e. those not starting with an underscore) except those who are explicitly excluded should be proxied by default (like it is done with hybrid properties), or whether there should be a Meta attribute with a list of properties that must be explicitly set.

We probably also need to add a way to specify which type the (normal or hybrid) properties shall be converted to. This could also be defined in a Meta attribute, or maybe we could check for Python type hints if nothing is defined, like this:
```python
    def full_name(self) -> str:
        return self.first_name + ' '  + self.last_name

    def current_age(self) -> int:
        return current_year() - self.year_of_birth
```

I am willing to contribute with code, but first I want to get some feedback regarding this idea from others.

issue_title: PIPELINE settings variable also used in python-social-auth causing a clash
issue_body:
The variable name `PIPELINE` is used by both this library and [python-social-auth ](https://github.com/omab/python-social-auth)

The clash takes place in `social.strategies.base.BaseStrategy.get_pipeline`



This breaks the python-social-auth library for Django, to temporarily resolve this I have used a monkey patch as follows:

```
from social.strategies import base
from social.pipeline import DEFAULT_AUTH_PIPELINE

def patch_social_pipeline(*args, **kwargs):
    return DEFAULT_AUTH_PIPELINE

base.BaseStrategy.get_pipeline = patch_social_pipeline
```

issue_title: Usability issue: clobbered runs, lost data
issue_body:
It's very natural to write code that writes multiple runs to the same run directory, e.g. because you have code that explicitly specifies a log directory, and you run that code multiple times. Empirically, it looks like TensorBoard silently discards some of the data so as to show only one run (?)

It would be great to solve this problem for our users. There are basically two approaches:

1. Change TensorBoard so that it automatically detects when the run has restarted, and separates it into a new run (probably with a numerical sequential naming scheme). 
1.1 Ideally, add on some API that makes it natural to give these runs names or choose to preserve certain runs. (We've talked about adding explicit hyperparameter support to TensorBoard, I think that would help here.)

2. Add a new SummaryWriterManager type API, which when used in lieu of the summary writer automatically creates a clean directory structure for TensorBoard.

I think 1. is much preferable to 2. because there are so many SummaryWriters/FileWriters already present in many different libraries that use TensorFlow, it would be difficult to get an API usage shift at this time. 

@jart is this something you could include in your backend data ingestion refactoring?

issue_title: Tensorboard (Embeddings Projector) not working with large checkpoint files
issue_body:
- TensorBoard version (from pip package, also printed out when running `tensorboard`): 1.7.0
 - TensorFlow version if different from TensorBoard: 1.7.0
 - OS Platform and version (e.g., Linux Ubuntu 16.04): macOS High Sierra Version 10.13.3
 - Python version (e.g. 2.7, 3.5): 3.6.5
 - For browser-related issues:
   - Browser type and version (e.g. Chrome 64.0.3282.140): Version 66.0.3359.139 (Official Build) (64-bit)
 
I am facing a problem with large checkpoint files when trying to visualize embeddings on Tensorboard.  This becomes a problem because a lot of the embeddings I am dealing with have a high number of features. The error that I get when running Tensorboard is:

> Error fetching tensors
> /Users/sakibarrahman/knowtions/elpis/static/tf_logs/tests/tensorboard_tests/tensorboard_test_model.ckpt-1.data-00000-of-00001; Invalid argument

Console:

> http://localhost:6006/data/plugin/projector/tensor?run=.&name=tensorboard_test&num_rows=100000
> Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 400 (BAD REQUEST)

Below is a simple script that reproduces the problem. Tensorboard runs perfectly fine if I just reduce the number of embeddings or change the number of features in the embeddings. This script produces large checkpoints (over 2GB). If I reduce the number of samples or the number of features in the embeddings (e.g. n_samples = 50000), Tensorboard works fine. Is there a size limit on Tensorboard or any way to override it? I could not find any mention of size limits in the documentations.

```
import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np
import os
from tensorflow.contrib.tensorboard.plugins import projector


name = "tensorboard_test"
LOG_DIR = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "static/tf_logs/tests/tensorboard_tests")
if not os.path.exists(LOG_DIR):
    os.makedirs(LOG_DIR)

NAME_TO_VISUALISE_VARIABLE = name

n_samples = 482493
n_features = 1370

path_for_metadata = os.path.join(LOG_DIR, name + '_metadata.tsv')
with open(path_for_metadata, 'w') as f:
    for i in range(n_samples):
        f.write(f"{repr(i)}\n")
print("Metadata file created")
arr_embeds = np.random.rand(n_samples, n_features)

#  Set to a tf variable
embedding_ph = tf.placeholder(tf.float32, arr_embeds.shape, name='embedding_place_holder')
embedding_var = tf.Variable(tf.zeros(arr_embeds.shape), name=NAME_TO_VISUALISE_VARIABLE)
var_init = embedding_var.assign(embedding_ph)
summary_writer = tf.summary.FileWriter(LOG_DIR)

#  Create projector
config = projector.ProjectorConfig()

#  Add embedding to the projector
embedding = config.embeddings.add()
embedding.tensor_name = embedding_var.name
embedding.metadata_path = path_for_metadata 
projector.visualize_embeddings(summary_writer, config)

print("Saving Data")
# Save the data
sess = tf.InteractiveSession()
sess.run(tf.global_variables_initializer())
sess.run(var_init, feed_dict={embedding_ph: arr_embeds})
saver = tf.train.Saver()
saver.save(sess, os.path.join(LOG_DIR, name + "_model.ckpt"), 1)
```

issue_title: MLE Announce processing corner case
issue_body:
Currently the Thread spec only specifies what to do if `local_timestamp > announce_timestamp` and what to do if `local_timestamp < announce_timestamp` The case where both timestamps are equal is unspecified.

OpenThread acts as if `local_timestamp > announce_timestamp` when both timestamps are equal. This causes a huge number of MLE Annouce Messages to be sent in rapid succession (see 
[InfiniteAnnounce.pcapng.gz](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/files/1138878/InfiniteAnnounce.pcapng.gz))

There is no behaviour specified in the Thread spec (raised the issue [SPEC-730](https://threadgroup.atlassian.net/browse/SPEC-730) to address this). I my opinion in this case it is not too bad to do nothing (no not apply the information from the MLE Announce and do not send any MLE announces yourself)

This would mean to split the `if ... else` into an `if ... elif ... else` construct in mle.cpp:3036. (I know `elif` does not exist in c++, but it explains it more clearly)
The above would address the issue in the short term (and is compliant with the spec), until the Thread group figures out what to do with the issue

issue_title: Inform radio layer about pending packets
issue_body: There is no functionality in which MAC layer would inform radio (implementation) layer about pending frames for a particular node. In this case implementation is forced to always set FRAME_PENDING bit in ACK frames. This is not ideal for sleepy devices, that will send DATA_REQUEST, receive an ACK with FRAME_PENDING bit set and wait for (probably) non-existent packet and waste battery life.

issue_title: Text labels in scatterplot not behaving as expected
issue_body: I am trying to create a scatterplot visualization of the embedding space for my vision model by looking at the t-SNE projections of a few classes. As far as the plotting goes, I seem to have everything wired up. In this attached image, you can see that I'm looking at a plot of dog and cat embeddings. The problem I am having is that the 'textlabels' that I have supplied the plot function are not showing up. My goal is to show the image id for the given data point, so that I can dive deeper into data points that are not where I'd expect them to be (e.g. a dog image deep within the cat cluster). I would expect the mouseover to show either the class and my supplied label, or at the very least, my supplied label. In the current implementation, it's instead only showing the class. The code supporting my visdom plot: ``` print('Computing t-SNE') # This will produce the (N, 2) coordinate matrix vis_pos = bh_sne(all_vecs) # This is the (N,) matrix of labels for each point vis_lab = numpy.concatenate([numpy.repeat(i+1, v.shape[0]) for i, v in enumerate(q_vecs)]) print('Done') print('Plotting...') opts = { 'legend': q_list, 'title': args.plot, # This is a python list of string ids for each image (size N) 'textlabels': all_ids, 'mode': 'markers+text' } print('vis_pos', vis_pos.shape) print('vis_lab', vis_lab.shape) print('all_ids', len(all_ids)) vis.scatter(X=vis_pos, Y=vis_lab, win=args.plot, opts=opts) ``` Sample Output: ``` Plotting... vis_pos (2000, 2) vis_lab (2000,) all_ids 2000 ``` Both my client and server version of visdom is 0.1.7 I was looking into the source code (master), and there appears to be a few bugs in at least the client code. I'll try to outline what I think I'm seeing, but my workaround also doesn't work. In this block: https://github.com/facebookresearch/visdom/blob/4d9f7c0e6a896943707fd255e0d46bc6798a42e3/py/__init__.py#L838-L872 It appears to be iterating over each class, and preparing the markers separately for the data for each respective class. The problem is, the function doesn't also select the relevant `textlabels` for the given class on line 854. The apparent workaround to this, just to see if I could get the textlabels to show was to set all of the points to the same class, which should cause the supplied labels to again align with the points. The problem is, I still don't see my supplied labels, as shown. Is there a setting that I am missing, or a known bug for this? I'm also happy to dig further into this if someone can point me in the rough direction of the server code that's making the plotly calls. Thanks

issue_title: GNU Readline goes haywire if prompt contains ANSI color codes
issue_body:
If at any point the cmd2 prompt gets changed to involve anything involving color via ANSI color escape codes, then the behavior of readline when the up and down arrows are pressed gets really messed up.

This is due to a bug in how GNU Readline calculates the prompt length when the Python3 input() or Python2 raw_input() methods are called.  This article has more info:
https://bugs.python.org/issue17337

Ideally, we can make cmd2 protect against this case, even though it is fundamentally a bug in GNU Readline.

issue_title: LogDNAHandler.emit fails with non-keyword arguments
issue_body:
Hi. I've tried to add LogDNAHandler to logging configuration of some existing project and noticed that it started raise a lot of exceptions. If the record was logged with non-keyword arguments, like

    logger.debug("User %s is now %s", username, status)

Then logger would fail with `TypeError: tuple indices must be integers or slices, not str`. Still, that's a valid and reasonably common way to use standard library logging.

This happens because:

    opts = {}
    if 'args' in record:
        opts = record['args']
    ...
    if 'level' in opts:
        message['level'] = opts['level']

And `record["args"]` is a list, e.g.:

    {
        "args": ["drdaeman", "active"],
        "message: "User drdaeman is now active",
        "msg": "User %s is now %s",
        "levelname": "DEBUG",
        "levelno": 10,
        ....
    }

I know, the structured logging and keyword arguments are the way to go, but legacy logging code and quick hacks (to print some debug data quick) are reality.

Also, I'm not sure it is a good idea to use `record.args` at all - in standard library's `LogRecord`, its sole purpose is to convert from `msg` to `message`. Maybe this logic should be put behind some `options["use_args"]` flag.

issue_title: Rename all backends to "EmailBackend" for consistency with Django
issue_body:
Django's [built-in email backend](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.10/topics/email/#email-backends) class names are all `EmailBackend`; the type of backend is indicated by the module:

* django.core.mail.backends.**smtp**.EmailBackend
* django.core.mail.backends.**console**.EmailBackend
* django.core.mail.backends.**filebased**.EmailBackend
* django.core.mail.backends.**locmem**.EmailBackend
* django.core.mail.backends.**dummy**.EmailBackend

Before 1.0-alpha, Anymail should change to follow Django's naming convention:

* anymail.backends.**mailgun.MailgunBackend** → anymail.backends.**mailgun**.EmailBackend
* anymail.backends.**mandrill. MandrillBackend** → anymail.backends.**mandrill**.EmailBackend
* anymail.backends.**postmark.PostmarkBackend** → anymail.backends.**postmark**.EmailBackend
* anymail.backends.**sendgrid.SendGridBackend** → anymail.backends.**sendgrid**.EmailBackend
* anymail.backends.**sparkpost.SparkPostBackend** → anymail.backends.**sparkpost**.EmailBackend

To avoid breaking code, the current names should still be supported with a deprecation warning.

(As an additional benefit, this would eliminate the need for admonishments throughout the docs on how to properly capitalize each ESP's name.)

issue_title: Catching error_already_set in QThread causes deadlock
issue_body:
I using Qt to create a QThread to hold an embedded python interpreter. When pybind11::eval throws an exception in this thread, the gil_scoped_acquire declaration in the destructor of error_not_set causes a deadlock on PyEval_AcquireThread().

I deleted the gil_scoped_acquire and everything works as expected. What is the purpose of having the acquire statement there in the first place and will I break something by deleting it?

issue_title: Implement DNS-based DAV service discovery
issue_labels: [{'id': 353602202, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwzNTM2MDIyMDI=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pimutils/vdirsyncer/labels/cat:%20RFCs', 'name': 'cat: RFCs', 'color': '5319e7', 'default': False}, {'id': 89425274, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw4OTQyNTI3NA==', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pimutils/vdirsyncer/labels/cat:%20server%20compat', 'name': 'cat: server compat', 'color': '5319e7', 'default': False}, {'id': 553518808, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw1NTM1MTg4MDg=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pimutils/vdirsyncer/labels/lv:%20medium', 'name': 'lv: medium', 'color': 'fbca04', 'default': False}, {'id': 90006607, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5MDAwNjYwNw==', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pimutils/vdirsyncer/labels/mod:%20DAV', 'name': 'mod: DAV', 'color': '207de5', 'default': False}, {'id': 344407071, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwzNDQ0MDcwNzE=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pimutils/vdirsyncer/labels/planning', 'name': 'planning', 'color': 'e6e6e6', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Currently we do the following in `dav/mod.rs` (after merging #747):

* if the URL configured by the user has a path != `"/"`, use it directly as homeset-URL (interpreting it as manual override)
* otherwise attempt to call `GET https://{host}:{port}/.well-known/caldav` (in `get_well_known_url`) and hope we will get redirected to the right URL
* if we get an error from the server, use the configured URL as base URL again

It would be nice if we got DNS-based discovery into our heuristics as well (see http://sabre.io/dav/service-discovery/). The biggest question is *when* we should do that. Two ideas come to mind:

## Just add it to the chain
* Attempt to use configured URI directly, if it works, return
* Attempt well-known-based discovery, if it works, return
* Attempt DNS-based discovery, if it works, return

## URL-less login

Or perhaps only attempt DNS-based discovery if the user didn't specify a URL and their username is an email address?

So this will trigger the current heuristics:

```
url = https://icloud.com
username = jeanne.doe@icloud.com
password = ...
```

But this will trigger DNS-based discovery:

```
username = jeanne.doe@icloud.com
password = ...

issue_title: Fatal warnings
issue_body:
As reported by @Mechazawa [here](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/commit/d2f1ae9c3870289b52fb1c0ab5130c8fae52aa72#commitcomment-18318527), if `*-auto` needs the `--debug` flag on your system, it will display a warning and then exits abnormally.

Since it causes the program to exit, it's not really a warning so we should not report it as such.

issue_title: certbot-auto re-installs virtualenv when plugin is causing issues
issue_body:
When running `certbot-auto`, and it tries to see if it needs to update itself, if a plugin is causing the `letsencrypt --version` to NOT return the version number, `certbot-auto` will not show any error, and will simply re-install the virtualenv completely.
I would expect certbot-auto to instead return the error returned by `letsencrypt --version`, and simply exit.
This will allow me to fix what is wrong with the installed plugin, or uninstall it.

issue_title: Proxy breaks relative URLs in proxied pages
issue_body:
Test setup / VM:
- Jupyter notebook on guest port `8888`, host port `35180`
- OpenRefine on guest `3334`
- `nbserverproxy` enabled
- OpenRefine proxied as `http://localhost:35180/proxy/3334`

The OpenRefine page doesn't render because the OpenRefine HTML includes relative links to assets:

```
...
<link type="text/css" rel="stylesheet" href="externals/select2/select2.css" />
 <link type="text/css" rel="stylesheet" href="externals/tablesorter/theme.blue.css" />
...
```
which resolve as e.g. `http://localhost:35180/proxy/externals/select2/select2.css` (404) rather than `http://localhost:35180/proxy/3334/externals/select2/select2.css` (which would resolve correctly)?

issue_title: Add ability to set aside a slice for all the single user servers
issue_body:
If we spawn all single user notebooks into a slice and set cpu / memory limits on that slice, we can guarantee that notebook users can never, individually or in aggregate, OOM a machine.

Ideally we would also be able to just create the slice transiently if it doesn't exist.
